title: Spermacoce chamissonis
taxonomy: Spermacoce chamissonis is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Spermacoce, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the false buttonwoods. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as the coffee or madder family. Spermacoce chamissonis is a species of flowering plant native to the Americas. Its classification is further detailed at the rank of species within the binomial nomenclature system.

Additionally, Spermacoce chamissonis is used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. side_effects: Spermacoce chamissonis is a plant species that is used in traditional medicine, but its use may have some side effects. intro: Spermacoce chamissonis, also known as Butterprint or False Buttonweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce chamissonis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
